content.method: Longitudinal cohort tracking using linked administrative education and labor-market/tax records, applying regression discontinuity around admission thresholds to isolate track effects from selection and background.
content.question: Do short-term value-added gains for students selected into 6/8-year gimnázium tracks persist into upper-secondary completion, tertiary attainment, and labor-market outcomes, or do they dissipate once background and peer effects are accounted for?
content.why_it_matters: This is central to assessing whether expanding access via quotas (T5) or maintaining the current track (T1) delivers durable benefit versus merely reallocating a contested/possibly illusory advantage.
